title: Switzerland Loses Top Competitiveness Ranking to Singapore
summary: Switzerland lost its position as the world’s most competitive economy to Singapore, slipping to third place in the ranking as high US trade tariffs and a strong Swiss franc hurt investment flows.

title: JPMorgan’s Ward Says Falling Oil Can Unleash European Stocks
summary: It’s time to buy European stocks, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. Asset Management’s Karen Ward.

title: China Stock Gauge Heads for Bear Market as Tech Weakness Extends
summary: A major Chinese stock gauge slid toward a bear market, pressured by weakness in Internet and consumer firms.

title: Tesco Sales Disappoint as Wetter Weather Deters UK Shoppers
summary: Tesco Plc’s sales rose less than expected in the fiscal first quarter as the UK’s biggest supermarket grapples with weaker consumer sentiment and a tough comparison with last year.

title: Russian Gasoline Prices Jump as Ukraine Strikes Refineries
summary: Russian gasoline prices advanced further, with Ukrainian attacks on refineries forcing some filling stations to limit sales just as the summer vacation season gets underway.

title: Fed Turns Hawkish, Spurs Surge in Rate Hike Bets | The China Show 6/18/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley, Temasek Set for Big Pay Day From NSE’s India IPO
summary: The National Stock Exchange of India Ltd.’s planned initial public offering is set to unlock the value of long-term investments for a group of backers including Morgan Stanley, Temasek Holdings Pte. and State Bank of India.

title: Traders See 40-Year Yen Low as Next Intervention Battleground
summary: Yen watchers are bracing for the currency to slide to its weakest level in four decades against the dollar, a move that many see as the next threshold for the authorities to intervene.

title: Indoor Playground Price Rises Add to Costs for British Families
summary: After recovering from the pandemic, soft-play centers are hiking prices to cushion the blow of soaring costs.

title: Asian Stocks Shrug Off Pressure from Hawkish Fed | The Asia Trade 6/18/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Sydney and Singapore with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Avril Hong, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic Ban Forces Investor Rethink of Political Risk
summary: Investors in the ever-hotter AI stock rally must suddenly consider a risk with the potential to be even more damaging than high valuations and big spending: Politics getting in the way.

title: Goldman Says Hormuz Oil Flows May Recover to Only 70% After War
summary: Oil flows going through the Strait of Hormuz may recover to only about 70% of their pre-war level, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., which highlighted regional producers leaning on alternative routes.

title: US Futures Climb as Iran Deal Offsets Hawkish Fed: Markets Wrap
summary: US equity futures jumped and oil prices retreated as President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, boosting risk sentiment after the Federal Reserve’s hawkish hold.

title: Oil Slides on Hormuz Deal as Brent Closes In on Losing War Gain
summary: Oil fell as an interim US-Iran peace deal went into effect, putting the focus on how quickly transits through the Strait of Hormuz can be ramped up as Persian Gulf producers try to restart shut-in fields.

title: Fed’s Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
summary: It didn’t take Kevin Warsh long to get the bond market’s attention.

title: Copper Falls After Fed Chairman Warsh Positions as Inflation Hawk
summary: Copper fell more than 1% to wipe out the gains of earlier this week after US Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh sparked speculation about rate hikes in comments at his debut press conference.

title: Sprake: SARB Likely to Hike Before Year-End
summary: Traders are paring back their bets on further interest-rate hikes by the South African Reserve Bank after inflation came in lower than expected. But Casey Sprake, Market Strategist at AG Capital told Bloomberg’s Chief Africa Correspondent Jennifer Zabasajja, they are expecting another rate hike before the end of the year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bohl: Iran Successfully Leveraged Hormuz In Negotiations
summary:
President Trump signed an MOU to end the war in Iran & reopen the Strait of Hormuz after more than 100 days of the conflict. Ryan Bohl, Senior Middle East and North Africa Analyst at RANE spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the difference between this deal and the JCPOA under the Obama administration.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Unemployment Falls, Wages Grow Faster Than Expected
Oil and Gas Ships Cross Strait of Hormuz After US-Iran Deal
summary: A laden liquefied natural gas carrier and an empty products tanker are crossing the Strait of Hormuz, as traders watch for signs of traffic through the key waterway after an interim US-Iran peace deal went into effect.

title: Kingboard’s 570% Rally Is Being Powered by Mainland Investors
summary: Chinese investors have fueled a surge in shares of newfound AI darling Kingboard Laminates Holdings Ltd., more than doubling their stake in the company to 13% this year, according to the latest stock exchange data.

title: US and Iran Sign Interim Peace Deal
summary: President Trump has signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, speeding up the timeline for the agreement to go into effect. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: WeRide to Accelerate European Expansion
summary: Tony Han, Founder & CEO of WeRide, says the company is planning to launch in more European cities after starting joint programs with Uber in Madrid and Zurich. He adds that he is optimistic that China will resume issuing new autonomous driving permits. He speaks exclusively on “Bloomberg: The China Show.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mukesh Ambani’s Promise to Investors on Jio IPO Will Be Tested This Week
summary: The long-running saga over the listing of Reliance Industries Ltd.’s digital unit comes to the fore this week, as investors watch whether its billionaire chairman Mukesh Ambani can walk the talk from last year.

title: Czechs Mull First Rate Hike Since 2022 to Curb Price Pressures
summary: The Czech central bank may raise interest rates for the first time in four years as policymakers weigh domestic inflation pressures against receding risks from global energy prices.

title: Shanghai Jiaotong University’s Zhu Ning on PBOC Policy
summary: Zhu Ning, Professor of Finance at the Shanghai Advanced Institute of Finance, says China is using overnight reverse repos to help guide policy rates and ease spikes in funding stress. He also says China’s credit slowdown is not necessarily negative, as the country is shifting its growth model. He speaks with Minmin Low on the sidelines of the Lujiazui Forum in Shanghai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tata Starbucks CEO To Add Up to 100 Stores
summary: Tata Starbucks CEO Sushant Dash plans to add up to 100 stores in the short term. In an exclusive conversation, Dash adds that they are firm on maintaining the market share of 30%. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rio Tinto Resumes Copper Exports From Massive Mongolian Mine
summary: Rio Tinto Group has resumed exports of copper concentrate from its vast Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia following a short protest.

title: India Monsoon Starts Almost 40% Short as El Niño Upends Weather
summary: India’s monsoon has had an unusually weak start and the lackluster rainfall is likely to persist, threatening crops and industrial activity as the El Niño weather phenomenon takes hold.

title: Brookfield Sells Wembley Stadium Builder Multiplex for $650 Million
summary: Brookfield Business Corp. has agreed to sell Multiplex, the construction company that built London’s Wembley Stadium, to Japan’s Obayashi Corp. for $650 million.
